## [00:00 - 03:05]

Alright guys let's talk about targeting when you're dealing with a local audience. So this could include things like a restaurant, a gym, a doctor, a chiro's, a movie theater, any local business that is targeting within a localized parameter. So Lee Jen is mostly made up of this kind of targeting although you do have some Lee Jen clients about 20% which do work with broader audiences and we'll cover that in the next video. So if you are working with sort of a Lee Jen client that does target sort of nationwide or across maybe even several countries just skip to the second video. This is for those of you working with a smaller local audience. Now this can be tricky sometimes because you are very much restricted to what's happening around you in terms of the population sizes, how many people are living in the city. So really with Lee Jen we try to keep things a little bit more simple and this is definitely the case if you compare it against sort of e-com where you would run lots of different tests like lookalikes and audiences 1 million, 2 million plus. With local Lee Jen often times you'll end up with audience sizes of around 60,000 to about 300,000 that's normally what the audience sizes will come down to. So really we try to keep things simple with our testing and what I would recommend you start with initially is what we call blanket marketing and this is basically you may have heard of this referred to as also carpet bombing but this is basically where you're just segmenting the age, the gender if that's applicable and any other demographic category. So for example if we're targeting brides we would simply just select people that just recently got women that recently got engaged between the age of say 25 to 65 and that would be our targeting it would be pretty blanket. Now that is probably going to be a fairly small audience as well and what you have to bear in mind here is that depending which city you're targeting so in New York City that might work really well but if you're working if your client is working you know in a small town you're going to have you know maybe even under a thousand people in that audience and it's not going to feed out very well. So you want to have a minimum audience size of around 30,000 to get started with if you run your ad sets so if that's the case just go super blanket females put in an age and that's it and that should be a much bigger audience. Now the other audience we want to test out against our blanket marketing is either interests or demographics or behavioral categories. So for example if you're targeting brides you could run another ad set testing you know

## [03:06 - 04:57]

the interests of bride and groom magazine or what's like a bride TV show say yes to the dress or something like that. So that could be an option as well. Now if your customer does if your client does have previous customer data I would also highly recommend testing out a look alike of their custom audience. So basically upload that customer list into Facebook and then create a look alike a 1% look alike from that audience and then segment it within the local area. Now you might be asking yourself well how far how far of a radius do I put out for my ads right how far of a radius this is a study that was done by Bright local and it does a really good job you can also find this in the you know beneath this video and it talks about some of the how far in terms of minutes someone is willing to journey based on the type of business. So for example a cake shop about 23 minutes the doctor about 21 minutes if you're looking at the pub 14 minutes yoga 12 and then again gym. So that gives you an idea that if you're working with a gym client don't go too broad with your radius because it's probably going to be shown to a lot of people that may opt in but then they start to realize how far this gym in is and they don't convert and that's going to frustrate your client because they're going to start saying well the quality is low or you know they're not within they're not coming in basically. So that's kind of an idea of how far to throw that web out. Now let's have a look at what to do when you're working with broader audiences and I'm going to show you how to use audience insights here too.
